Bash(bash /home/jycxed/Documents/weshake/api-1/.claude/skills/ticket/scripts/create_ticket.sh \\ "[Employee] Restrict access rights before onboarding API call" \\ '

Description

When creating or updating an employee with want_a_card: true, the function startOnboardingAndRestrictAccess\(\) in service.employee.js applied the access rights restriction \(buildDashboardOnlyAccessRights\(\)\) after the external Ubble onboarding API call, inside a Promise.all.

The problem: both callers \(creation at line 292 and update at line 397\) wrap the call in a try/catch that logs the error and silently swallows it. If the API call failed \(owner not onboarded, service unavailable, etc.\), the restriction was never applied and access_rights remained NULL.

Security impact: the permission system interprets access_rights = NULL as full access to all modules. An employee with want_a_card: true could therefore gain full platform access if the external API call failed.

Root cause

In startOnboardingAndRestrictAccess\(\), the call to updateOne\(USER, employeeUser.id, { access_rights: buildDashboardOnlyAccessRights\(\) }\) was bundled in a Promise.all with the notification creation, after the call to startEmployeeOnboardingAndVerification\(\). Any error from the API call prevented the Promise.all from executing, leaving the restriction unapplied.

Fix

Move the updateOne\(USER, ...\) call with buildDashboardOnlyAccessRights\(\) before the external API call, so the employee is locked down to dashboard-only access immediately, regardless of whether the Ubble API call succeeds or fails. The Promise.all is also simplified to a plain await create\(NOTIFICATION, ...\) since it only contains a single operation.

Files to modify

FileReason
src/module/employee/service.employee.jsMove the updateOne call with buildDashboardOnlyAccessRights\(\) before startEmployeeOnboardingAndVerification\(\) in startOnboardingAndRestrictAccess\(\) \(line 69\). Simplify the Promise.all to a single await.

Test plan

  • Create an employee with want_a_card: true → verify access_rights in DB is { dashboard: true, ... all others false }
  • Simulate an external onboarding API failure → verify access_rights is still restricted
  • Verify the full onboarding flow still works end-to-end when the API is available

Related PR: #996

')

Bash(bash ./scripts/create_ticket.sh "[Chat] Use authUserId in message-chat controller so staff act as themselves, not owner" "

Same bug pattern as WESHAKEV2-116 / PR #1026.

The JWT middleware sets req.user to the owner user for staff tokens \(loaded via decode.owner_id || decode.id\) while req.authUserId holds the actual logged-in user ID. The message-chat controller passes req.user directly to the service, and the service writes/reads by user.id \(user_send_id, send_by_user_id\). Result: when a staff member sends or reads messages, records are attributed to their owner instead of themselves.

Fix

Replace req.user with { ...req.user, id: req.authUserId } in every handler of src/module/message-chat/controller.message-chat.js \(sendMessage, updateMessage, deleteMessage, getMessageById, getListMessages, viewMultiMessage\) to keep the full user context but scope the id to the real authenticated user. Mirror exactly the pattern used in src/module/notification/controller.notification.js.

Files to Modify

FileReason
src/module/message-chat/controller.message-chat.jsReplace req.user with { ...req.user, id: req.authUserId } in all 6 handlers.

Related: WESHAKEV2-116, PR #1026 \(commit f6fc991b\).

")

Bash(bash ./scripts/create_ticket.sh "[Chat] Use authUserId in group-chat controller so staff act as themselves, not owner" "

Same bug pattern as WESHAKEV2-116 / PR #1026.

The JWT middleware sets req.user to the owner user for staff tokens while req.authUserId holds the actual logged-in user ID. The group-chat controller forwards req.user to the service, which persists/filters by user.id as member_id. Staff members therefore join and read group chats as their owner instead of themselves, leaking cross-user data and breaking per-staff membership and unread counts.

Fix

Replace req.user with { ...req.user, id: req.authUserId } in every handler of src/module/group-chat/controller.group-chat.js: createGroupChat, updateGroupChat, updateSupportGroupChat, deleteGroupChat, getGroupChatById, getListGroupChats, getListMembersByGroupChat, viewGroupChat, createGroupChatWithAdmin, countGroupUnreadByUser, getGroupChatWithAdmin, checkGroupChatWithUserById, getContacts. Mirror the pattern already used in src/module/notification/controller.notification.js.

Files to Modify

FileReason
src/module/group-chat/controller.group-chat.jsReplace req.user with { ...req.user, id: req.authUserId } in all handlers so member scoping uses the real authenticated user.

Related: WESHAKEV2-116, PR #1026 \(commit f6fc991b\).

")

Bash(bash ./scripts/create_ticket.sh "[Settings] Use authUserId in setting controller so staff see their own settings" "

Same bug pattern as WESHAKEV2-116 / PR #1026.

The JWT middleware sets req.user to the owner user for staff tokens while req.authUserId holds the actual logged-in user ID. The setting controller forwards req.user to the service, which reads/writes by user_id: user.id. As a result, staff members read and overwrite their owner's personal settings instead of their own.

Fix

Replace req.user with { ...req.user, id: req.authUserId } in every handler of src/module/setting/controller.setting.js: updateSetting, getSettingById, checkBanKeywords, setBanKeywords. Mirror the pattern used in src/module/notification/controller.notification.js.

Files to Modify

FileReason
src/module/setting/controller.setting.jsReplace req.user with { ...req.user, id: req.authUserId } in all 4 handlers so personal settings are scoped to the real authenticated user.

Related: WESHAKEV2-116, PR #1026 \(commit f6fc991b\).

")
